labels.rationale: Evidence is only the title-like span 'Governed Collaborative Memory as Artificial Selection'; public asset is GAIA; endpoint is GAIA Level 1 success rate; hypothesis is that governed memory improves task success over unfiltered shared vector memory. The runnable shape is clear, but the problem grounding and task subset are too thin for A.
labels.repair_needed: Add a concrete failure-mode span and restrict the GAIA pilot to memory-dependent tasks with a fixed multi-agent protocol.

labels.rationale: Evidence identifies MANTRA trace-check granularity as useful for debugging failure modes; public assets include the MANTRA dataset and linked code; endpoint is a benchmark delta versus the reported protocol; hypothesis is that a granularity ablation reveals or improves agent debugging. It is public and grounded, but the actual ablation and metric are placeholders.
labels.repair_needed: Specify the ablation, such as per-constraint failure attribution accuracy versus an LLM-judge baseline, before running.

labels.rationale: Evidence states that assign-then-route heuristics get trapped by decoupling allocation and routing; public artifact is RL4CO/CVRP-style tooling; endpoint is optimality gap or total path cost; hypothesis is that joint autoregressive decoding beats decoupled assignment-routing. The idea is measurable, but the baseline is inconsistent across K-means plus LKH, OR-Tools, and CPLEX, and the first pilot is synthetic rather than tied to a public split.
labels.repair_needed: Choose one public CVRP/MARL routing benchmark and one fixed baseline, then define the exact small-size pilot.

labels.rationale: Evidence says MANTRA empirically shows trace-level check granularity can debug agent failures; public assets are the MANTRA dataset and linked code; endpoint is benchmark delta versus the reported protocol; hypothesis is that a granularity ablation measures the failure mechanism. The candidate is duplicative and still lacks a concrete intervention.
labels.repair_needed: Turn the debugging-granularity claim into a precise ablation with named trace-check levels and a diagnostic metric.

labels.rationale: Evidence states exact solvers can take hours or days and the proposed method claims large speedups; public artifact is tsplib-python plus implied RL4CO models; endpoint is time-to-solution with optimality gap; hypothesis is that neural inference crosses under one second while retaining solution quality. It needs a public solver/model setup because IBM CPLEX and pretrained AM artifacts are not pinned.
labels.repair_needed: Replace the licensed CPLEX baseline with a public OR-Tools or Concorde-style baseline and identify the exact pretrained neural solver artifact.

labels.rationale: Evidence only says MANTRA has limitations and future research; public assets include MANTRA links; endpoint is a generic benchmark delta; hypothesis is the templated claim that 'future research' is measurable. This is not a defined scientific intervention.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing concrete limitation, intervention, and measurable hypothesis.

labels.rationale: Evidence directly states sparse fixed graphs can hide non-edge diversity when agents form clusters; public artifact is the Graph-SND repository; endpoint is MAPE against full SND; hypothesis is that task-adaptive or cluster-aware edges reduce global SND estimation error at fixed edge budget. Baseline and 24-72h pilot are concrete.

Evidence says MANTRA overcomes limitations by synthesizing compliance benchmarks; public assets are MANTRA links; endpoint is generic benchmark delta; hypothesis is a templated stress-test claim. The span is a solution claim, not a failure mode to test.
labels.fatal_blocker: No stated limitation or concrete mitigation target is identified.

labels.rationale: Evidence says all RL runs use IPPO and centralized critics are future work; public artifact is the Graph-SND code; endpoint is throughput-adjusted reward and mean episode reward; hypothesis is that Graph-SND regularizes MAPPO without full all-pairs diversity cost. Baseline MAPPO with full SND and a small 10-agent pilot are executable.

Evidence names UniPrefill future work on decoding acceleration or training-time efficiency; public artifact is the UniPrefill repository; endpoint is currently just accuracy; hypothesis is a templated broader-inference ablation. The metric and intervention do not match the stated speedup direction.
labels.repair_needed: Pick one dimension, such as decoding acceleration, and register TTFT, tokens per second, and RULER accuracy as endpoints.

labels.rationale: Evidence says EPITIME future work includes heterogeneous populations; public artifacts are EPITIME and public mixing-matrix data; endpoint is final-size error plus positivity and conservation; hypothesis is that a multi-group NSFD renewal solver preserves structure better than Euler/trapezoid baselines. The two-group contact-matrix pilot is bounded and executable.

Evidence identifies sparse-attention acceleration as tightly coupled to full attention; public artifact is UniPrefill; endpoint is generic benchmark delta; hypothesis is that an ablation around this coupling changes performance versus the reported protocol. The mechanism is real, but the proposed ablation is not specified.
labels.repair_needed: Define a concrete coupling test, for example disabling block-wise sparsification at specific attention stages and measuring TTFT, memory, and RULER accuracy.

labels.rationale: Evidence names vaccination dynamics coupled with information-dependent behavioral responses and memory effects; public assets are EPITIME and OWID COVID vaccination data; endpoint is MSE of vaccination rate over time; hypothesis is that a distributed-delay memory kernel explains uptake waves better than Markovian SIRV feedback. The first 24h solver change is concrete.

Evidence says prefill methods are incompatible with continuous batching; public artifact is UniPrefill; endpoint is quality-cost frontier; hypothesis is that stress-testing continuous batching exposes a measurable mechanism. This is high-value, but 'stress-test and mitigate' is not yet a specific implementation or workload.
labels.repair_needed: Define a vLLM-style continuous-batching workload, concurrency levels, and metrics for TTFT, throughput, and RULER accuracy before running.

labels.rationale: Evidence says HOIs can be reproduced by effective pairwise models and cannot generally be inferred from time series alone; public artifact is a GLV simulator; endpoint is post-perturbation MASE or identifiability score; hypothesis is that perturbation responses reveal HOI signatures absent from pairwise fits. The three-species simulation pilot is executable.

Evidence only says Hygieia has limitations warranting discussion; public artifact is a Hygieia repository; endpoint is generic benchmark delta; hypothesis is a templated limitation claim. No actual limitation, ablation, or disease benchmark split is specified.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing concrete failure mode and endpoint despite a public code link.

labels.rationale: Evidence is only 'This article is currently under review' and a broad origin-of-life title; public artifact is Avida; endpoint is selection coefficient or relative fitness; hypothesis is an invented threshold for pre-biotic transitions. The proposal is not grounded in a stated paper gap.
labels.fatal_blocker: No usable problem evidence or paper-grounded hypothesis.

labels.rationale: Evidence states protein methods fail to achieve function and foldability simultaneously; public data is only described generically and no code or dataset URL is provided; endpoint is generic benchmark delta; hypothesis is templated. A 24-72h path is not safe without public artifacts and a concrete ablation.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing public artifact URL and concrete intervention.

labels.rationale: Evidence names counterexamples with non-orchard networks; public artifact is PhyloNetworks.jl; endpoint is isomorphism discrimination rate; hypothesis is that augmenting mu-representation with additional path counts distinguishes non-orchard networks. The baseline mu-distance and small counterexample pilot are clear.

Evidence discusses alternative readings of an OLMo evaluation-context result; public data is only generic referenced benchmarks with no URL; endpoint is generic benchmark delta; hypothesis is templated. The ablation is unspecified and cannot be run safely from the provided assets.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing public protocol artifact and concrete ablation.

labels.rationale: Evidence states cut-edge-transfer distance is NP-hard while the new mu-representation has polynomial cost; public artifact is PhyloNetworks.jl; endpoint is wall-clock time for a distance matrix plus cluster silhouette; hypothesis is that mu-distance lowers posterior-analysis overhead without losing cluster resolution. Synthetic network generation and timing are executable in 24-72h.

Evidence names OLMo eval-cautious heterogeneity and judge dependence; public data is only generic referenced benchmarks; endpoint is generic benchmark delta; hypothesis is templated. No concrete judge, prompt set, model list, or artifact is pinned.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing public artifact and executable protocol.

labels.rationale: Evidence says Euclidean connectivity ignores functional barriers such as highways and rivers; public artifacts are PB-DOM plus comparable wolf occupancy code; endpoint is WAIC or out-of-sample colonization AUC; hypothesis is that resistance connectivity improves wolf range prediction over Euclidean distance. The least-cost-path pilot is concrete.

Evidence says LatentRAG future work could use reinforcement learning for exploration and exploitation; public assets include FlashRAG datasets, FAISS, and model links; endpoint is generic and partly mismatched safety success rate; hypothesis is templated. The RL objective and multi-hop QA metric are not concrete.
labels.repair_needed: Define the RL reward, dataset subset, and endpoints such as exact match, F1, retrieval count, and latency.

labels.rationale: Evidence states environmental effects are estimated only in accessible sites and may extrapolate poorly; public assets are PB-DOM and GBIF otter data; endpoint is PMSE or occupancy probability in known unsuitable urban sinks; hypothesis is that a habitat-suitability prior prevents high urban occupancy artifacts. The MaxEnt-prior pilot is bounded.

Evidence describes LatentRAG itself as the proposed solution to a limitation; public assets include several dependency and dataset links; endpoint is generic benchmark delta; hypothesis is templated. The candidate does not identify a new failure mode or ablation beyond restating the paper's method.

Evidence is the title connecting IBD tracts and ROH for Ne inference; public asset is 1000 Genomes, with msprime implied for simulation; endpoint is relative error or MSE in Ne; hypothesis is that a joint IBD plus ROH coalescent likelihood reduces recent-generation variance. It is plausible, but the first pilot is synthetic and the estimator implementation is underspecified.
labels.repair_needed: Pin a public simulated benchmark protocol and specify the joint likelihood or estimator equations before running.

labels.rationale: Evidence states only 1-3 percent of token positions change and top-5 targeted corrections recover RL gains; public assets include ReasonMaxxer and related reasoning-RL repositories; endpoint is accuracy; hypothesis is a templated ablation around sparse policy selection. The intended intervention is close but still not specified as a reproducible protocol.
labels.repair_needed: Define the exact model, math benchmark, corrected-token selection rule, and random-control comparison.

labels.rationale: Evidence says epistatic strength, modularity, and heterogeneity shape local optima; public artifact is NK-Landscapes; endpoint is number of local optima or distance to global optimum; hypothesis is that modularity hurts non-population search. The proposed 1000-bit local-optima enumeration is not a 24-72h executable path.
labels.repair_needed: Scale the pilot to exhaustive N up to about 24 or use a pre-registered sampling estimator for larger N.

labels.rationale: Evidence states oracle intervention with random controls shows specific token choices causally recover RL gains; public assets include ReasonMaxxer and related reasoning-RL repos; endpoint is quality-cost frontier; hypothesis is templated. The causal claim is good, but the ablation protocol and benchmark are not pinned.
labels.repair_needed: Register a small model, benchmark, token-intervention rule, and accuracy-cost endpoint before running.

labels.rationale: Evidence says heterogeneous fitness removes the pre-replicative equilibrium; public asset is only the paper's synthetic m-ary genealogy model with no code URL; endpoint is TV distance or log-ratio divergence; hypothesis is a critical replication intensity. It is scientifically plausible but weakly benchmarked and not tied to a public artifact.
labels.repair_needed: Add a public implementation or exact parameter file for the core ODE system and define a small reproducible benchmark.
labels.fatal_blocker: No concrete public artifact beyond a described synthetic model.

labels.rationale: Evidence says agent loops may fail to preserve unaffected work or artifact consistency; public asset is only a generic author-provided repository with no URL; endpoint is generic benchmark delta; hypothesis is templated. The ablation and artifact are both missing.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing public artifact URL and concrete benchmark protocol.

labels.rationale: Evidence says future work should weaken dominance assumptions or use sharper spectral conditions; public artifact is the paper's ODE system; endpoint is spectral gap or convergence rate; hypothesis is that stability persists under negative spectral radius. The direction is concrete, but the parameter grid and falsification criterion are not pinned enough for A.
labels.repair_needed: Specify the truncated system size, parameter grid, and pass/fail stability criterion.

labels.rationale: Evidence says incoherent state may affect future revisions; public asset is generic author-provided code with no URL; endpoint is generic benchmark delta; hypothesis is templated. The candidate lacks a public executable benchmark and a specific lineage intervention.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing public artifact and concrete intervention.

labels.rationale: Evidence says constructed networks may be structurally complex and future work should target level-1 or galled-tree classes; public asset is Open Tree of Life for taxa and constraint generation; endpoint is reticulation index; hypothesis is that level-1 realizability follows restricted graph conditions. The brute-force small-taxa pilot is executable.

Evidence names non-deterministic execution in agent loops; public asset is generic author-provided code with no URL; endpoint is generic benchmark delta; hypothesis is templated. Without an artifact or deterministic graph protocol, this cannot be independently run.
labels.fatal_blocker: Missing public artifact URL and concrete executable path.

labels.rationale: Evidence only says interactive and embeddable phylogenetic tree visualization; public asset is Open Tree of Life; endpoint is rendering latency, FPS, and node-overlap ratio; hypothesis is that WebGL layout improves large-tree interaction over SVG. The benchmark is measurable, but the Heat-tree code artifact and exact test tree are not pinned.
labels.repair_needed: Add the Heat-tree repository or package version and a fixed 5000-node Newick benchmark file.

labels.rationale: Evidence says future work should extend the coordination-aware protocol to controlled variants while preserving diagnostics; public artifact is the coordination_aware_MARL repository; endpoint is generic benchmark delta; hypothesis is that controlled variants reveal coordination mechanisms. The factors and diagnostics are not specified.
labels.repair_needed: Name the controlled factors, such as agent count or action choices, and register return plus process-level coordination metrics.

labels.rationale: Evidence calls for complexity characterizations of price discovery in distributed systems; public artifacts are the Trading Agent Competition repo and Mesa; endpoint is communication complexity and convergence time to epsilon-equilibrium; hypothesis is that limited network connectivity changes scaling versus a centralized Walrasian baseline. The graph-density pilot is directly executable.

notes: Applied A only when public artifact, metric, baseline, hypothesis, and a bounded 24-72h path were already concrete.
Templated candidates with generic 'pre-registered benchmark delta' and unspecified ablations were downgraded to B or Reject depending on whether one concrete repair would make them runnable.
Execution-lineage and several paper-ablation candidates lacked public artifact URLs and were rejected despite interesting evidence spans.
